name: Morning Priorities
summary: Build day with no fires — focus on content production, hub cleanup, and Family tab spec expansion.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Morning team. Wednesday, February 18th. Light overnight data — no critical fires. Fear & Greed index is sitting at 38, so sentiment is cautious. Hub has one uncommitted change lingering. Lever app has 7 faith lessons live. Family tab spec is at 455 lines. Let's run through domains quick and get Joshua's day locked in. Elon, start us off.

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: Morning. One uncommitted hub change — I'll get that committed today, trivial. More interesting is the Family tab spec at 455 lines. That's getting substantial enough that we should draft a proper feature spec for the next expansion before it becomes unwieldy. I'll also review the family touchpoint tracker design for the 3 family members we're tracking. No blockers on my side.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Fear & Greed at 38 — we're in fear territory, not extreme but worth watching. I'll keep monitoring that throughout the day. On the revenue side, nothing actionable overnight. No new conversion signals to chase. Honestly, this is a good day for the content team to build up assets we can monetize later rather than pushing any aggressive offers into a fearful market.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Agreed with Dave — perfect build day. I've got three content items queued: drafting the next blog post, generating the daily devotional across all 8 faith dimensions, and drafting a new faith lesson for the Lever app to push us from 7 to 8 lessons. Also flagging — Jett's vaccine is coming up, so I'll prep a reminder for Joshua on that. Four deliverables from me today, all agent-handled.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: That's a full plate, Alex. Quick cross-talk — anyone have concerns or dependencies?

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: Alex, on the faith lesson draft — if you're building lesson 8, keep the data schema consistent with what's in the Lever app already. I don't want to retrofit anything. Ping me if the structure needs to change.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Noted. I'll match the existing schema exactly. If I see any friction I'll flag it before committing anything.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: One thought for Joshua — we're at 7 lessons now, and at some point we need to decide what the target lesson count is before we think about packaging and pricing tiers. That's a strategic call, not something we should just drift into.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good call, Dave. I'll surface that as a CEO decision. Alright, here's the wrap. Today is a build day — no fires, market is cautious, so we focus on content production and infrastructure cleanup. Elon commits the hub change and specs out the Family tab expansion. Alex produces three content pieces and preps the family reminder. Dave monitors sentiment. The one decision we need from Joshua is the Lever app lesson count target so Dave can start thinking about monetization tiers. Let's execute.

priorities: Draft faith lesson 8 and daily devotional content for Lever app
Draft Family tab feature spec expansion and review touchpoint tracker
Monitor Fear & Greed index at 38 and hold on aggressive revenue plays

action_items.text: Generate daily devotional content based on 8 faith dimensions
Draft faith lesson content for the Lever app (currently 7 lessons)
Prepare reminders for upcoming family events: Jett's vaccine
Draft feature spec for expanding Family tab (currently 455 lines)
Review family touchpoint tracker design for 3 family members

ceo_questions.question: What is the target lesson count for the Lever app faith module before we lock pricing tiers?
ceo_questions.context: We're at 7 lessons and actively building more — Dave needs a target to design monetization packaging.
options: Keep building organically and decide later
